context: Agent applies the failure-focused Must test to two contested features and produces an arbitration recommendation.

checklist.name: Failure-focused test question stated
checklist.description: conflict-resolution.md explicitly states the failure-focused test question ('Does the release fail without this?') before applying it.

checklist.name: Feature A correctly resolved as Must or Should with evidence
checklist.description: conflict-resolution.md applies the test to Feature A and references the 40% abandonment rate as the deciding evidence (Must is defensible given conversion impact).

checklist.name: Feature B correctly resolved as Could or Should (not Must)
checklist.description: conflict-resolution.md applies the test to Feature B and concludes that brand feel / UX polish does not constitute release failure, placing it in Could or Should.

checklist.name: Stakeholder communication language provided
checklist.description: conflict-resolution.md includes specific language or talking points for explaining each decision to the affected stakeholder (Marketing VP and UX lead).

checklist.name: Anti-pattern rule cited
checklist.description: conflict-resolution.md explicitly references the skill rule that stakeholder preference is not the same as release-critical need when addressing Feature B.
